_note: Quick-links sidebar + in-app reference pages. To add a new external resource: append to `resources` with type 'pdf' or 'link' and a `url`. To add a new in-app reference: add an entry to `resources` with type 'in-app' and matching `internal_route`, plus the page content in `in_app_pages` keyed by id. Resources with `pin_to_categories` also appear as pinned cards on those category views in the main guideline section.

alfred-intubation-checklist.title: Intubation checklist
alfred-intubation-checklist.subtitle: Alfred ICU · pre-intubation safety
alfred-intubation-checklist.warning: Two airway-competent doctors MUST attend. CICO = can't intubate, can't oxygenate — proceed to cricothyroidotomy.

items.text: Is there risk of physiological compromise?
Are the ICU consultant and ANUM aware?
Do anaesthetics or ENT need to be contacted?
What are the plans for anticipated physiological compromise?
What are Plans A, B, C & D if the airway is difficult?
What drugs / doses are to be given? When and how?
Does anyone have questions or concerns?

alfred-intubation-checklist.source_label: Alfred ICU Airway Team via intensiveblog.com

attribution.upstream: Modified from the RTIC Severn Checklist

alfred-intubation-checklist.footer_note: In-app copy provided under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Do not use commercially. Verify against the source card before clinical use.

anticoag-reversal-hi.title: Anticoag reversal in head injury
anticoag-reversal-hi.subtitle: Adult patient with intracranial haemorrhage on CT
anticoag-reversal-hi.warning: Consult ARV (Adult Retrieval Victoria) before administering any reversal agent. Always check the live REACH source — doses may have been updated.

trigger.primary: Adult on oral anticoagulant or antiplatelet
trigger.secondary: Head injury · ICH confirmed on CT

classes.label: Warfarin
classes.subtitle: Vitamin K antagonist (VKA)

steps.title: Prothrombinex-VF (PCC)
steps.note: First-line. Reverses elevated INR within 15 min. Use the maximum 50 u/kg dose if bleeding is life-threatening.

steps.title: Vitamin K
steps.note: Sustains the reversal achieved by PCC (PCC has a short half-life). IV onset faster than oral.

steps.title: Add FFP
steps.note: FFP not routinely needed in isolated TBI — only add when bleeding is life-threatening.

steps.title: FFP (substitute)
steps.note: Allow time for blood typing (or use group AB), thawing, and infusion.

classes.after: Restart anticoagulation only after neurosurgical discussion.

steps.title: Idarucizumab (Praxbind)
steps.note: Given as 2 × 2.5 g IV infusions, each over 5–10 min, no more than 15 min apart. Effect immediate; lasts ~24 h. Requires haematology approval.

steps.title: Limited stock (Victoria)
steps.note: Idarucizumab is only stocked at The Alfred and Royal Melbourne (Major Trauma Centres). Plan for transfer if not on-site.

steps.title: Haemodialysis
steps.note: Consider if excessively prolonged APTT (>80 s), dabigatran level >500 ng/mL, or impaired renal function.

steps.title: PCC (Prothrombinex-VF)
steps.note: PCC is NOT suggested for direct thrombin inhibitors (dabigatran).

steps.title: No direct antidote listed by REACH
steps.note: Andexanet alfa is not listed in the REACH pathway. Focus is on resuscitation, factor replacement, and adjuncts below.

steps.title: Tranexamic acid (TXA)
steps.note: Continue infusion until bleeding controlled. Evidence limited — do not delay resuscitation.

steps.title: Recombinant factor VIIa (NovoSeven)
steps.note: Off-label. Prerequisites before use: Hct >24%, pH >7.2, platelets >50 × 10⁹/L, fibrinogen >1.0 g/L. Follow local protocol; haematology discussion.

steps.title: PCC (Prothrombinex-VF)
steps.note: Cannot fully reverse Xa inhibitors. May act as partial bypass agent at high doses. Off-label; thrombotic risk.

steps.title: FEIBA
steps.note: Off-label use; thrombotic risk unclear. Considered for life-threatening bleeding when other options unavailable.

classes.label: Antiplatelet
classes.subtitle: Lower bleed risk than oral anticoagulants

steps.title: Platelet transfusion
steps.note: Suggested for patients on antiplatelet therapy when significant bleeding, or platelets <50 × 10⁹/L.

steps.title: No pharmacological reversal listed
steps.note: REACH does not specify an antiplatelet reversal regimen for head injury. Supportive care, local haemostasis, and surgical/radiological intervention as indicated.

global_steps_before.title: Initial workup
items: CT brain — perform on every anticoagulated head injury patient
FBC, coagulation studies, INR (if on warfarin), creatinine
Blood group and hold + antibody screen
Consider oral charcoal if oral anticoagulant ingested <2 hours prior

anticoag-reversal-hi.footer_note: Pathway is decision support, not a protocol. Always verify current doses at the REACH live source and discuss with ARV / haematology / neurosurgery before administration.
